name: Body Pose Tracking & Rep Counting
promptInjection:
CAPABILITY: Body Pose Tracking (Vision Framework)
When building rep counting or body pose detection with the camera placed in front of the user:

ARCHITECTURE:
- Use AVCaptureVideoDataOutput (NOT photo output) to get continuous CMSampleBuffer frames.
- Process frames with VNDetectHumanBodyPoseRequest on a dedicated processing DispatchQueue.
- Throttle processing to ~15fps — use a frame counter and skip frames to avoid overloading. Not every frame needs analysis.
- Keep AVCaptureSession setup in a separate CameraManager class, pose processing in a separate PoseDetector class.
- The CameraManager must use AVCaptureVideoDataOutput with setSampleBufferDelegate on a processing queue.
- CameraManager must have an onFrame closure that PoseDetector subscribes to for receiving CMSampleBuffer frames.
- CameraManager must conform to AVCaptureVideoDataOutputSampleBufferDelegate.
- Include CameraPreviewView (UIViewRepresentable wrapping AVCaptureVideoPreviewLayer) for showing the camera feed.
- Use session preset .medium (not .high) — pose detection does not need high resolution and lower resolution improves performance.
- ALL THREE FILES (CameraManager.swift, CameraPreviewView.swift, PoseDetector.swift) must be created. Do not reference CameraManager without creating it.

POSE DETECTION:
- From VNHumanBodyPoseObservation, extract joint points using recognizedPoint(forGroupKey:jointKey:) — e.g. .bodyLandmarkRegionKeyLeftShoulder is NOT valid. Use the VNHumanBodyPoseObservation.JointName enum: .nose, .neck, .leftShoulder, .rightShoulder, .leftElbow, .rightElbow, .leftWrist, .rightWrist, .leftHip, .rightHip, .leftKnee, .rightKnee, .leftAnkle, .rightAnkle.
- Filter joints by confidence > 0.3 before using.
- Vision coordinates are normalized (0–1) with origin at bottom-left. Shoulder Y DECREASES as the person lowers toward the ground.

REP COUNTING — ADAPTIVE THRESHOLD APPROACH (critical for correctness):
Do NOT use hardcoded threshold values (e.g. 0.55, 0.45). Different camera angles, distances, and body proportions produce completely different metric ranges. Hardcoded thresholds cause either stopwatch-like phantom counting or no counting at all.

Instead, use ADAPTIVE THRESHOLDS based on a sliding window of recent metric values:
1. Keep a sliding window of the last ~60 smoothed metric samples (~4 seconds at 15fps).
2. Compute the window's min and max each frame.
3. If the range (max - min) is less than 0.08, the person is STILL — do NOT count. Show a status like 'Start moving...'.
4. When range >= 0.08, compute dynamic thresholds:
   - downThreshold = windowMin + range * 0.35
   - upThreshold = windowMax - range * 0.35
5. These thresholds automatically adapt to any camera angle, distance, or body type.

SETUP DELAY:
- After the body is first detected, wait 2 seconds before any counting logic runs. Show 'Get ready...' during this time.
- This gives the user time to set the phone down and get into position.
- After the delay, start collecting metrics but only count once sufficient motion is detected (range >= 0.08).

STILLNESS DETECTION:
- If the sliding window range is below 0.08, the person is not moving enough to be doing reps. Skip all counting logic.
- This prevents phantom counting when the user is resting, adjusting position, or setting up.
- Resume counting automatically once motion is detected again.

STATE MACHINE:
- Exactly 2 states: idle and wentDown.
- idle: metric drops below dynamic downThreshold → enter wentDown.
- wentDown: metric rises above dynamic upThreshold AND at least 0.8 seconds since last rep → count +1, return to idle.
- One full down-up cycle = exactly one rep. Never count on the down transition.
- Cooldown of 0.8 seconds between reps (allows up to ~75 reps/min which covers fast exercisers).

SMOOTHING:
- Apply exponential moving average: smoothed = alpha * raw + (1 - alpha) * previous.
- Alpha = 0.25. This balances responsiveness with noise rejection.

STATUS MESSAGES (publish to UI):
- 'Get ready...' — during the 2-second setup delay after body detection.
- 'Start moving...' — body visible but not enough motion to count (range < 0.08).
- Empty string '' — actively counting reps.
- These status messages should be shown in the UI below or near the rep counter.

UI REQUIREMENTS:
- Show a large rep counter (at least 80pt font, .bold) readable from 6–8 feet away.
- Show the camera preview behind the counter using CameraPreviewView (UIViewRepresentable wrapping AVCaptureVideoPreviewLayer).
- Show the statusMessage from PoseDetector when non-empty.
- Include a 'body not visible' warning when no pose is detected for 2+ seconds.
- Include a manual +1 button as fallback.
- Provide Start/Stop workout controls.

CRITICAL ANTI-PATTERNS:
- NEVER use hardcoded threshold values (0.55, 0.45, 0.60, 0.35, etc.) — they only work for one specific camera setup and fail for everyone else. Always use adaptive thresholds from a sliding window.
- NEVER count reps when the user is still — always check that the metric range exceeds a minimum before entering counting logic.
- NEVER start counting immediately when 'Start Workout' is pressed — add a 2-second setup delay so the user can get into position.
- NEVER use Core Motion for camera-in-front setups — the phone is stationary, accelerometer reads nothing useful.
- NEVER count on both the down-phase and up-phase — one full cycle = one rep.
- NEVER process every single frame — throttle to ~15fps or the UI will stutter.
- NEVER run VNImageRequestHandler on the main thread.
- NEVER forget to set the frame orientation: VNImageRequestHandler(cmSampleBuffer: buffer, orientation: .right) for front camera in portrait mode.
- NEVER use a smoothing alpha above 0.3 — too reactive, allows noise to trigger false state transitions.
- NEVER add StoreKit, AppStore, or subscription code unless the user explicitly asks for in-app purchases. Exercise trackers do not need monetization code.
- NEVER reference a class (like CameraManager) without creating the file that defines it.
antiPatterns: Using hardcoded threshold values (0.55, 0.45, 0.60, 0.35, etc.) — these only work for one camera setup; use adaptive thresholds from a sliding window instead
Counting reps when the user is still or not moving enough — always check sliding window range >= 0.08 before counting
Starting to count immediately on Start Workout — need a 2-second setup delay for the user to get into position
Using Core Motion for camera-in-front exercise counting (phone is stationary — use Vision pose instead)
Counting on BOTH the down-phase AND up-phase of the motion — one full down-up cycle must equal exactly one rep
Using a smoothing alpha above 0.3 — too reactive, allows metric noise to trigger false state transitions
Processing every camera frame without throttling (causes UI stutter)
Running VNImageRequestHandler on the main thread
Forgetting orientation parameter in VNImageRequestHandler for front camera
Using incorrect joint name enums (use VNHumanBodyPoseObservation.JointName, e.g. .leftShoulder, .rightShoulder)
Referencing CameraManager without creating the file that defines it
Adding StoreKit/AppStore/subscription code when the user didn't ask for it

commonErrors: Stopwatch-like phantom counting when still (v2: hardcoded thresholds too wide for user's camera setup)
